floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: judge's diagnosis fully endorsed -- the `v0++; v0--;` pair (src/main.c:2440-2441) is INERT dead weight, constant-folded to nothing before any codegen-relevant pass, and the target e

frontier.hypothesis: Deleting `v0++; v0--;` (leaving `else { v0 = 0; }`) is byte-identical: sandbox 0, retire clean, queue done.
frontier.mechanism: cse.c const-prop + flow.c DCE erase the pair before jump2/sched2/reorg ever see it; the target else-arm is already the folded form.
frontier.next_probe: Delete src/main.c:2440-2441; `sandbox func_8008AE7C --disable all` (expect 0); verify-oracle --rebuild; queue done func_8008AE7C.

frontier.hypothesis: If (against prediction) a diff appears, it is confined to early jump.c branch-sense/threading on the if-else chain (the only pre-cse pass that saw the extra statements), not to the join or stores.
frontier.mechanism: jump.c first pass runs before cse and keys some invert/thread decisions off arm shape; all later passes see identical RTL.
frontier.next_probe: Read the masked diff; if branch-sense flipped, consult switch-vs-ifchain-branch-sense before touching structure -- and note the finding would upgrade this construct from 'inert' to 'load-bearing dead code', a dead-store-fake-exception ruling candidate.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Dead paired inc/dec `v0++; v0--;` in the else branch (src/main.c:2440-2441) has no semantic purpose — the pair cancels out (v0=0 before and after) and GCC 2.7.2 constant-folds it away, emitting no corresponding instructions. The target ASM for that branch is just the bne delay slot `addu $v0,$zero,$zero`. The construct fails Test 1 (byte-identical behavior with or without it) and Test 2 (no human programmer writes a self-cancelling inc/dec from a spec). Fix: remove `v0++;` and `v0--;`, leaving `else { v0 = 0; }`. The byte-match is preserved because GCC was already discarding these instructions.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
